title: conmon process missing, cannot retrieve exit code: conmon process killed
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Sometimes containers like envoy or traefik simply stop listening and clients get connection refusals, their entries are missing in `ss -tlpn`, but when I try to restart them, I get the following error message `Error: container c6e6b4a6b8389f948f5ad09a06ed1cefe9c46bdedc8c03d83ba9d65f9a33c1ca conmon process missing, cannot retrieve exit code: conmon process killed`. Also wasn't able to find anything helpful in the system journal.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Currently no idea, but could be caused by `--net=host`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

comments.body:
Upd: `podman ps` reports that container is up and running, but it's not, the process cannot be found in the system's `ps`

`journalctl CONTAINER_NAME=...` doesn't contain anything helpful.

```
$ podman inspect -f '{{json .State}}' envoy
{"OciVersion":"1.0.2-dev","Status":"running","Running":true,"Paused":false,"Restarting":false,"OOMKilled":false,"Dead":false,"Pid":1389,"ConmonPid":1386,"ExitCode":0,"Error":"","StartedAt":"2021-12-27T08:36:09.436192442Z","FinishedAt":"2021-12-20T18:02:49.278545383Z","Healthcheck":{"Status":"","FailingStreak":0,"Log":null}}
```

```
$ ls /proc/1386
ls: cannot access '/proc/1386': No such file or directory
```

Turns out `podman-restart` killed the container: 
```
systemd[1]: podman-restart.service: State 'stop-sigterm' timed out. Killing.
systemd[1]: podman-restart.service: Killing process 1386 (conmon) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[1]: podman-restart.service: Failed with result 'timeout'.
systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always.
```

This is a bin confusing, because all containers successfully started and only then podman-restart timed out.
